failed.title: Matrix includes required column: Obligation Phase
failed.match: PASS if the matrix/table includes a column categorizing each requirement as pre-submission, pre-issuance, or post-issuance (or equivalent phase classification). FAIL if no such column exists.

failed.title: Matrix includes required column: Responsible Party
failed.match: PASS if the matrix/table includes a column identifying the responsible party or action owner for each requirement. FAIL if no such column exists.

failed.title: Matrix includes required column: Deadline/Trigger
failed.match: PASS if the matrix/table includes a column for the deadline or trigger event for each requirement. FAIL if no such column exists.

failed.title: ISSUE_002: Recommends disclosure with protective cover letter
failed.match: PASS if the agent recommends full disclosure of Okonkwo's expunged conviction while also submitting a cover letter or accompanying explanation citing the ordinance's expungement protection, or recommends raising the inconsistency with the City to avoid scoring prejudice. FAIL if neither approach is recommended.

failed.title: ISSUE_005: Identifies 50% R3 hiring as binding post-issuance obligation
failed.match: PASS if the agent identifies the social equity requirement that at least 50% of Greenleaf's workforce must be hired from R3-designated census tracts within Evanston, and characterizes this as a binding ongoing/post-issuance compliance obligation subject to audit. FAIL if this requirement is not identified or not characterized as ongoing.

failed.title: ISSUE_005: Notes specific hiring numbers (6 of 12 employees)
failed.match: PASS if the agent notes that with 12 projected full-time employees, at least 6 must be R3 residents at all times (12 × 50% = 6), or otherwise quantifies the obligation based on the projected workforce. FAIL if the specific numerical obligation is not calculated or mentioned.

failed.title: ISSUE_005: Flags permit revocation consequence for non-compliance with R3 hiring
failed.match: PASS if the agent flags that failure to maintain the 50% R3 hiring threshold could result in permit revocation or jeopardize social equity status. FAIL if no consequence for non-compliance is mentioned.

failed.title: ISSUE_006: Notes mentorship overlap with annual permit renewal
failed.match: PASS if the agent identifies that the 2-year mentorship program spans across permit renewal periods (annual renewal at 12 months), and that social equity status is re-verified at each renewal, meaning failure to remain in the mentorship program could jeopardize the social equity designation at the first renewal. FAIL if this timing overlap and renewal risk is not flagged.

failed.title: ISSUE_007: Flags Ramachandran residency and social equity qualification
failed.match: PASS if the agent flags that Felix Ramachandran resides in Wilmette (not Evanston), holds 40% ownership, and that the agent recommends verifying whether the City's ordinance or Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act requires all owners above a certain threshold to individually qualify for social equity status, or whether Okonkwo's 60% qualifying ownership is sufficient. FAIL if Ramachandran's non-Evanston residency is not identified as a potential issue for social equity qualification.

failed.title: ISSUE_009: Flags difficulty obtaining cannabis crime/theft coverage
failed.match: PASS if the agent flags that the required $500,000 cannabis-specific crime/theft insurance coverage is difficult to obtain because standard commercial crime policies typically exclude cannabis businesses due to federal Schedule I status, and that specialized cannabis insurers must be used. FAIL if the agent does not flag the difficulty or limited market for this specific coverage.

failed.title: ISSUE_010: Identifies abbreviated first reporting period
failed.match: PASS if the agent identifies that quarterly compliance self-audit reports are due within 15 days of each calendar quarter end, and that if the permit is issued mid-quarter (e.g., mid-August), the first audit will cover only a partial quarter (e.g., August 15 – September 30), with results due October 15. The agent should recommend establishing audit procedures immediately upon permit issuance. FAIL if the abbreviated first reporting period issue is not identified.

failed.title: Identifies secure vestibule entry with ID verification
failed.match: PASS if the matrix includes the requirement for a secure vestibule entry with ID verification before access to the retail floor. FAIL if this requirement is omitted.

failed.title: Identifies special use approval / ZBA process requirement
failed.match: PASS if the matrix includes the requirement for special use approval from the Zoning Board of Appeals (ZBA) for a cannabis dispensary in the C1a Commercial Mixed-Use district, including the multi-step process (application with site plan, notice to property owners within 500 feet, public hearing, ZBA recommendation, City Council vote). FAIL if the special use / ZBA requirement is omitted.

failed.title: Identifies Liquor & Cannabis Board public hearing requirement
failed.match: PASS if the matrix includes the requirement for a public hearing before the Liquor & Cannabis Board within 45 days of application completeness determination. FAIL if this requirement is omitted.

failed.title: Identifies quarterly self-audit reporting obligation
failed.match: PASS if the matrix includes the ongoing requirement for quarterly compliance self-audits with results submitted to the City within 15 days of each calendar quarter end. FAIL if this requirement is omitted.

failed.title: Identifies Ridgeline Community Bank quarterly attestations
failed.match: PASS if the matrix includes the requirement for quarterly compliance attestations to Ridgeline Community Bank as part of the cannabis banking program. FAIL if this banking compliance obligation is not mentioned.

failed.title: Correctly categorizes requirements by obligation phase
failed.match: PASS if the matrix contains requirements classified across all three phases: pre-submission, pre-issuance, and post-issuance/ongoing. FAIL if all requirements are lumped into a single phase or if any of the three phases is entirely absent.

turns.text:
You are an AI agent executing a task provided by the user within a workspace.

## Workspace layout

Everything you work with lives under one workspace root. **`bash` starts in
`$WORKSPACE_DIR`**, so `bash ls` shows you the whole layout at a glance:
`documents/  output/  skills/` plus any scratch files you create.

- **`$WORKSPACE_DIR`** — your working area, default `bash` cwd. Use it for
  notes, intermediate files, and skill output. Skill scripts live at
  `$WORKSPACE_DIR/skills/<name>/scripts/`.
- **`$DOCUMENTS_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/documents`) — task documents.
  Read-only.
- **`$OUTPUT_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/output`) — deliverables. The harness
  routes relative `write` and `edit` paths here automatically.
- **Task configuration** (`task.json`) — contains the task definition and the
  grading rubric. Do not read, search, or reference it. Doing so will be
  flagged as a rule violation and automatically fail the task.

## Tool conventions

- Use `read` to consume input files (handles .docx, .xlsx, .pptx, .pdf, and
  plain text).
- Use the file-type skill manuals below to produce binary deliverables
  (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx).
- Use `write` only for plain markdown — typically a `response.md`
  summarizing your work.
- Use `edit` for incremental refinement of a file you have already created.

## Approach to the work

Before drafting, work the matter methodically. These steps apply to any
legal deliverable; adapt the emphasis to the task.

1. **Read every document fully, then fix the scope.** `read` each file in
   `$DOCUMENTS_DIR` completely — decisive facts hide in late schedules,
   exhibits, defined-term sections, and worked tables, not just the
   narrative. Then determine, from the task's framing plus the operative
   transactional/instruction documents, **exactly

**[CITY OF EVANSTON, ILLINOIS]{.underline}**

**[MUNICIPAL CODE]{.underline}**

**Title 9 --- Licenses, Permits, and Business Regulations**

**Article 5 --- Cannabis Business Establishments**

Adopted by the City Council of the City of Evanston, Cook County, Illinois, Ordinance No. 78-O-23, effective January 1, 2024, as amended. This Article is adopted pursuant to the authority granted to the City of Evanston under the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act, 410 ILCS 705/, and in the exercise of the City\'s home rule powers under Article VII, Section 6 of the Illinois Constitution of 1970. Codified at Title 9, Chapter 9, Article 5 of the Evanston City Code.

Right-click to update Table of Contents

**[Section 9-5-1. Title and Purpose]{.underline}**

**(A)** This Article shall be known and may be cited as the \"City of Evanston Cannabis Business Establishments Ordinance.\"

**(B)** The purpose of this Article is to establish comprehensive local regulations governing the permitting, licensing, operation, and oversight of cannabis business establishments within the corporate limits of the City of Evanston, consistent with and supplementary to the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (410 ILCS 705/) and the implementing rules and regulations adopted thereunder by the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation, the Illinois Department of Agriculture, and other applicable State agencies.

**(C)** The specific goals and objectives of this Article are as follows:

> \(1\) To protect the public health, safety, and general welfare of the residents and visitors of the City of Evanston;
>
> \(2\) To prevent the sale, distribution, or dispensing of cannabis and cannabis-infused products to persons under twenty-one (21) years of age;
>
> \(3\) To promote social equity and ensure that individuals and communities disproportionately impacted by cannabis prohibition and enforcement have meaningful opportunities to participate in the legal cannabis industry within the City;
>
> \(4\) To minimize adverse impacts of cannabis business establishments on surrounding residential neighborhoods, commercial districts, schools, daycare facilities, and other sensitive land uses;
>
> \(5\) To ensure the orderly and responsible integration of cannabis business establishments into the commercial fabric of the City of Evanston, in a manner that complements existing land uses and contributes positively to the economic vitality of the community;
>
> \(6\) To establish clear and transparent standards for the application, evaluation, issuance, renewal, and revocation of local cannabis business establishment permits;
>
> \(7\) To promote environmental sustainability in the operation of cannabis business establishments; and
>
> \(8\) To generate revenue for the City of Evanston that supports community reinvestment, restorative justice programs, workforce development, and public health initiatives, particularly in communities designated as Restorative, Reinvest, and Renew (R3) zones.

**(D)** This Article shall be liberally construed to effectuate the foregoing purposes. In the event of any ambiguity, the provisions of this Article shall be interpreted in favor of protecting public health and safety.

**[Section 9-5-2. Definitions]{.underline}**

For the purposes of this Article, the following terms shall have the meanings ascribed to them in this Section. Terms not defined herein shall have the meanings assigned to them in the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (410 ILCS 705/) or, if not defined therein, their common and ordinary meaning.

**(A) \"Agent\"** means any officer, director, manager, employee, or volunteer of a cannabis business establishment who is twenty-one (21) years of age or older and has been issued a valid Illinois Cannabis Dispensing Organization Agent Identification Card by the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation. The term \"Agent\" includes both full-time and part-time employees and any person who performs services for or on behalf of the cannabis business establishment in a capacity that involves the handling, sale, or dispensing of cannabis or cannabis-infused products.

**(B) \"Agent-in-Charge\"** means the individual designated by a cannabis dispensary as the on-site manager responsible for day-to-day operations and compliance with this Article and all applicable State law during all hours in which the dispensary is open for business. The Agent-in-Charge shall be a licensed agent who has been authorized by the dispensary\'s principal officers to act on behalf of the establishment in matters of regulatory compliance, security, and customer service.

**(C) \"Applicant\"** means any person, corporation, limited liability company, partnership, or other business entity that has filed or intends to file an application for a cannabis business establishment permit under this Article. The term \"Applicant\" includes all principal officers and any person who holds a financial or voting interest of five percent (5%) or greater in the applying entity.

**(D) \"Calendar Quarter\"** means the standard calendar quarters of each year, defined as follows: Quarter 1 (January 1 through March 31), Quarter 2 (April 1 through June 30), Quarter 3 (July 1 through September 30), and Quarter 4 (October 1 through December 31). All references in this Article to quarterly obligations, reports, or deadlines shall be calculated based on these fixed calendar quarter periods.

**(E) \"Cannabis Business Establishment\"** means a cultivation center, craft grower, infuser organization, dispensing organization, processing organization, or transporting organization, as those terms are defined in the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (410 ILCS 705/), or any successor legislation. The term \"Cannabis Business Establishment\" encompasses both adult-use and medical cannabis operations authorized under State law.

**(F) \"Cannabis Dispensary\"** or **\"Dispensing Organization\"** me

**CITY OF EVANSTON** *Office of the City Manager* *Department of Community Development*

**Cannabis Dispensary Permit** **Application Checklist and Submission Guide**

Issued by the Department of Community Development Cannabis Licensing Division

Pursuant to Evanston Municipal Code Chapter 9, Article 5

Lorraine H. Morton Civic Center 2100 Ridge Avenue, Evanston, Illinois 60201

**Cannabis Licensing Coordinator:** Renata Vasquez-Morris **Phone:** (847) 448-4311 \| **Email:** rvasquezmorris@cityofevanston.org

**Publication Date:** January 15, 2024 **Effective for Application Window:** February 1, 2024 through May 31, 2024

**[Section 1: General Instructions and Application Overview]{.underline}**

This guide accompanies the City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application Form and provides detailed instructions for completing and submitting a complete application package. All prospective applicants for a local cannabis dispensary permit are advised to review this guide in its entirety, along with Evanston Municipal Code Chapter 9, Article 5, before beginning the application process.

Applications for a Cannabis Dispensary Permit will be accepted during the open application window from **February 1, 2024 through May 31, 2024**. No applications will be accepted before or after this window. Applications will be processed in the order received.

The City targets a 90-day review period from the date a complete application is accepted. Following completeness determination, a public hearing before the Liquor & Cannabis Board will be scheduled within 45 days. After the public hearing, the Zoning Board of Appeals will issue its recommendation, and final action by the City Council is required. Applicants should plan for a total processing timeline of approximately 120 to 150 days from submission to final permit issuance, depending on scheduling and any requests for additional information.

**Incomplete applications** will receive a written deficiency notice. Applicants will have **fourteen (14) calendar days** from the date of the deficiency notice to cure all identified deficiencies. If deficiencies are not cured within this period, the application will be deemed withdrawn and the application fee will not be refunded.

**Submission Requirements:** All application materials must be submitted in hard copy (**three collated sets**) and one electronic copy (**USB drive, PDF format**) to:

> Department of Community Development Lorraine H. Morton Civic Center 2100 Ridge Avenue, Evanston, IL 60201
>
> Attention: Renata Vasquez-Morris, Cannabis Licensing Coordinator

A **non-refundable application fee of \$5,000.00** must accompany the application. Payment must be made by cashier\'s check or money order payable to \"City of Evanston.\" Personal checks and credit cards are not accepted.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to review Evanston Municipal Code Chapter 9, Article 5 in its entirety before beginning the application. The City is available for pre-application conferences by appointment.

**[Section 2: Applicant Eligibility Requirements]{.underline}**

The following eligibility requirements must be met by all applicants at the time of application submission:

**Entity Status.** The applicant must be a licensed business entity in good standing in the State of Illinois. Sole proprietorships are not eligible to hold a cannabis dispensary permit.

**State License.** The applicant must hold a valid State of Illinois conditional or permanent adult-use dispensing organization license issued pursuant to the Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act (410 ILCS 705/). The State license number and expiration date must be provided with the application. Applicants holding conditional licenses should refer to Checklist Item 3 for additional documentation requirements.

**Principal Officer and Owner Disclosure.** All principal officers, board members, and persons holding five percent (5%) or more ownership interest in the applicant entity must be identified in the application. Each such individual must authorize and undergo a fingerprint-based background check through the Illinois State Police and the Federal Bureau of Investigation. The following constitute disqualifying offenses: (a) any felony conviction within the preceding ten (10) years; (b) any felony conviction for a controlled substance offense, regardless of when the conviction occurred; (c) any conviction for a sex offense, regardless of when the conviction occurred; and (d) any conviction for a violent felony, regardless of when the conviction occurred. Expunged or sealed convictions are addressed in Evanston Municipal Code Section 9-5-22(D)(3); applicants should consult that provision carefully.

**Social Equity Eligibility.** Applicants may qualify for social equity designation and a 20-point scoring bonus if at least fifty-one percent (51%) of the qualifying ownership interest is held by one or more individuals who: (a) are residents of R3 (Restorative, Reinvest, Renew) designated census tracts within the City of Evanston; or (b) have a prior cannabis-related arrest, conviction, or adjudication of delinquency. Social equity applicants must complete the Social Equity Documentation requirements described in Checklist Item 18 and the detailed disclosure sections of the Application Form.

**[Section 3: Required Submission Components --- Master Checklist]{.underline}**

The following checklist identifies every document and item that must be included in the application package. Items marked with an asterisk (**\\***) must be submitted at the time of application. Items marked with a dagger (†*\*) must be submitted prior to permit issuance but are not required at the time of application. Applicants should include a cover sheet indexing all submitted materials by checklist item number.

**Item 1 --- Completed Application Form (\\\*)** Completed City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application Form (all sections, signed and notarized by an authorized repre

**\[OFFICIAL SEAL OF THE CITY OF EVANSTON\]**

**[CITY OF EVANSTON]{.underline}**

**[CANNABIS DISPENSARY PERMIT APPLICATION]{.underline}**

**Department of Community Development --- Cannabis Licensing Division**

Lorraine H. Morton Civic Center 2100 Ridge Avenue Evanston, Illinois 60201

**Cannabis Licensing Coordinator:** Renata Vasquez-Morris

Phone: (847) 448-8163

Email: rvasquez-morris@cityofevanston.org

**Application Window: February 1, 2024 through May 31, 2024**

**Application Fee: \$5,000.00 (Non-Refundable)** Payable by Certified Check or Wire Transfer to City of Evanston

Form Version 3.2 \| Revised January 12, 2024

**This application must be completed in its entirety. Incomplete applications will not be accepted. All information provided is subject to verification by the City of Evanston Department of Community Development, Cannabis Licensing Division, and such other City departments and agencies as may be necessary to evaluate the application.**

**[GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS AND IMPORTANT NOTICES]{.underline}**

**READ CAREFULLY BEFORE COMPLETING THIS APPLICATION**

All applicants for a City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit must carefully review and comply with the following instructions. Failure to comply with any instruction may result in the application being deemed incomplete or rejected.

1\. **Completeness Requirement.** All fields in this application must be completed or marked \"N/A\" if a particular field is not applicable to the applicant. Do not leave any field blank. Applications with blank fields will be returned without processing.

2\. **Submission Format.** This application must be submitted in hard copy (three (3) complete, bound copies, including all attachments and exhibits) and one (1) electronic copy on a USB flash drive in PDF format. All documents must be clearly legible and organized with tabs corresponding to each section and exhibit of the application. Hard copies and the USB drive must be delivered in person to the Cannabis Licensing Division at the Lorraine H. Morton Civic Center, 2100 Ridge Avenue, Evanston, Illinois 60201, during regular business hours (Monday through Friday, 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM).

3\. **Application Fee.** A non-refundable application fee of Five Thousand Dollars (\$5,000.00) must accompany the submission of this application. Payment must be made by certified check, cashier\'s check, or wire transfer payable to the \"City of Evanston.\" Personal checks, credit cards, and cash payments will not be accepted. The application fee covers the administrative costs of processing, reviewing, and evaluating the application and is non-refundable regardless of whether the application is approved, denied, or withdrawn.

4\. **Annual Permit Fee.** Upon issuance of a cannabis dispensary permit, the permittee must pay an annual local permit fee of Fifteen Thousand Dollars (\$15,000.00). The annual permit fee is due and payable at the time of permit issuance and at each annual renewal thereafter.

5\. **Performance Bond or Letter of Credit.** A performance bond or irrevocable letter of credit in the amount of Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$50,000.00) must be submitted prior to permit issuance. The bond or letter of credit must name the City of Evanston as the obligee or beneficiary and must remain in full force and effect for the duration of the permit term.

6\. **Processing Timeline.** The City will process applications in the order received. The City\'s target is to complete the initial review of each application within ninety (90) days from the date the application is determined to be complete. The 90-day review period begins upon the date of the formal completeness determination, not the date of submission. Applicants will receive written notice of the completeness determination. The City does not guarantee processing within the 90-day target period and shall not be liable for any delays.

7\. **Public Hearing.** A public hearing before the Evanston Liquor and Cannabis Board is required for each application. The public hearing will be scheduled within forty-five (45) days of the completeness determination. Applicants will receive written notice of the hearing date and must attend or send an authorized representative. The Liquor and Cannabis Board will make a recommendation to the City Council regarding approval or denial of the application.

8\. **Special Use Approval.** Cannabis dispensaries proposed for locations within C1a Commercial Mixed-Use zoning districts require special use approval from the Zoning Board of Appeals and a final vote of approval by the Evanston City Council. Applicants proposing locations in C1a districts should apply for special use approval concurrent with this permit application to avoid processing delays. Special use applications are filed separately with the Zoning Division of the Department of Community Development.

9\. **State License Requirement.** All applicants must hold a valid State of Illinois conditional or permanent adult-use dispensing organization license issued by the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation at the time of application submission. Applications from entities that do not hold a valid state license will not be accepted.

10\. **Municipal Code Reference.** Applicants are strongly encouraged to review City of Evanston Municipal Code Chapter 9, Article 5, which contains the full regulatory requirements governing cannabis dispensaries within the City. The Municipal Code is available online at the City\'s website or for review in person at the City Clerk\'s office. This application form is a supplement to, and not a replacement for, the requirements set forth in the Municipal Code.

11\. **Public Records Disclosure.** This application and all materials submitted with it are public records subject to disclosure under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. Information specifically exempt from disclosure under state law (e.g., Social Security num

**[GREENLEAF THERAPEUTICS LLC]{.underline}**

**[Business Plan: Executive Summary and Operations Plan]{.underline}**

**Prepared in Support of City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application**

*\[Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC Logo\]*

**Proposed Dispensary Location:** 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B Evanston, IL 60201

**Date:** April 2024

**CONFIDENTIAL AND PROPRIETARY**

**Contact:** Dara Okonkwo, Managing Member Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC dokonkwo@greenleaftherapeuticsllc.com (872) 555-0193

**[1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY]{.underline}**

Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC (\"Greenleaf\" or the \"Company\") intends to open and operate a retail adult-use cannabis dispensary at 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, Evanston, IL 60201, serving both adult-use recreational customers and registered medical cannabis patients in full compliance with all applicable State of Illinois and City of Evanston regulations. The Company\'s mission is to establish a safe, welcoming, and community-oriented dispensary that provides high-quality cannabis products while reinvesting meaningfully in the communities most impacted by cannabis prohibition.

**Company Formation.** Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC is an Illinois limited liability company formed on March 14, 2023, under the Illinois Limited Liability Company Act. The Company\'s Employer Identification Number is 88-3921047. Greenleaf\'s registered agent is Northwest Statutory Services, located at 205 N. Michigan Ave., Suite 810, Chicago, IL 60601.

**Ownership.** Greenleaf is owned by two members: Dara Okonkwo, who holds a 60% membership interest and serves as Managing Member, and Felix Ramachandran, who holds a 40% membership interest as a Silent Member. Total initial capitalization of the Company is \$1,850,000, comprising \$1,110,000 contributed by Okonkwo and \$740,000 contributed by Ramachandran. All capital has been contributed as equity, and the Company carries no debt financing.

**State Licensing Status.** Greenleaf holds State of Illinois Conditional Adult-Use Dispensing Organization License No. ADC-285-0147, issued September 22, 2023, with a one-year conditional term expiring September 21, 2024. Pursuant to State requirements, conditional licenses must be converted to permanent status within 180 days of local permit issuance. The Company anticipates submitting its City application by May 15, 2024, and based on the City\'s target 90-day review period, expects to receive its local permit in time to commence the 180-day conversion process well before the conditional license expiration. Greenleaf is prepared to fulfill all State conversion requirements promptly upon receipt of the local permit.

**Local Application.** Greenleaf is filing its application under the City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit program. The City\'s application window opened on February 1, 2024, and the deadline for submission is May 31, 2024. Greenleaf is targeting submission on or before May 15, 2024, to allow maximum processing time within the City\'s review cycle.

**Social Equity Designation.** Greenleaf\'s Managing Member, Dara Okonkwo, qualifies as a social equity applicant under the Illinois Cannabis Regulation and Tax Act. Okonkwo is a resident of a census tract (8092.02) within Evanston that has been designated as an R3 (Restorative, Reinvest, Renew) area. Additionally, Okonkwo has a prior cannabis-related conviction --- a 2016 misdemeanor for possession of cannabis in an amount less than 30 grams, adjudicated in Cook County, Illinois, which was subsequently expunged in 2020. As a social equity applicant holding a majority ownership position, Greenleaf intends to claim the 20-point social equity bonus on the City\'s 250-point scoring rubric, for a maximum possible score of 270 points.

**Market Opportunity.** The City of Evanston currently has one licensed cannabis dispensary: Prairie Wellness Dispensary, located at 807 Church St., approximately 2,100 feet from Greenleaf\'s proposed location. Greenleaf\'s position on Sherman Avenue --- a high-foot-traffic commercial corridor --- provides meaningful geographic differentiation. The Company projects Year 1 gross revenue of \$3,200,000, based on conservative assumptions regarding customer traffic, average transaction value, and product mix that are detailed in Section 4 of this business plan.

**Key Advisors.** Greenleaf has assembled an experienced team of professional advisors to support its application and operations:

> • **Legal Counsel:** Hargrove & Sinclair LLP, 77 W. Wacker Dr., Suite 3200, Chicago, IL 60601 (partner Nadia Petrova, IL Bar #6312441; associate Jordan Mackey, IL Bar #6425098)
>
> • **Security and Compliance Consultant:** Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC (principal Marcus Wren)
>
> • **Cannabis Banking Partner:** Ridgeline Community Bank, 820 Davis St., Evanston, IL 60201

**Build-Out and Opening.** Greenleaf estimates a total build-out cost of \$425,000 for the proposed dispensary location. The build-out scope includes construction of a secure vestibule entry, hardened secure storage room, retail floor installations, office and employee areas, HVAC system upgrades, surveillance system installation, and ADA-compliant accessibility throughout. The Company targets a build-out period of approximately 90 days following local permit issuance, with an additional 30 days allocated for systems testing, staff training, and final regulatory inspections. Greenleaf anticipates opening for business within 120 days of receiving its City of Evanston dispensary permit.

This business plan sets forth in detail the Company\'s organizational structure, proposed facility, market analysis, operations plan, financial plan, social equity and diversity commitments, community engagement activities, regulatory compliance framework, and risk mitigation strategies. Greenleaf is committed to operating a best-in-class dispensary that serves the Evanston community responsibly and contributes to the City\'s goals for equitable cannabis commerce.

**[SUMMARY OF KEY LEASE TERMS]{.underline}**

**[Commercial Lease Agreement]{.underline}**

**Prepared for Inclusion in the City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application**

**Prepared by:** Hargrove & Sinclair LLP, 77 W. Wacker Dr., Suite 3200, Chicago, IL 60601

**Prepared for:** Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC

**Date of Summary:** April 2024

This summary extracts key terms from the executed Commercial Lease Agreement dated January 8, 2024 (the \"Lease\" or \"Lease Agreement\"), between Kimball Properties Inc. (\"Landlord\") and Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC (\"Tenant\"). This summary is provided for informational and regulatory application purposes only and does not supersede, amend, or modify the full Lease Agreement in any respect. In the event of any inconsistency between this summary and the terms of the executed Lease, the terms of the executed Lease shall control. All capitalized terms used but not defined herein shall have the meanings ascribed to them in the Lease Agreement.

**[Section 1: Parties to the Lease]{.underline}**

**Landlord:** Kimball Properties Inc., an Illinois corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of Illinois, with its principal offices located at 2200 Central St., Suite 400, Evanston, IL 60201. Kimball Properties Inc. is the fee simple owner of the building located at 1420 Sherman Ave., Evanston, IL 60201, as more particularly described in Section 11 below.

**Tenant:** Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC, an Illinois limited liability company, formed on March 14, 2023, under the Illinois Limited Liability Company Act (805 ILCS 180/). Tenant\'s Employer Identification Number (EIN) is 88-3921047. Tenant\'s registered agent for service of process is Northwest Statutory Services, located at 205 N. Michigan Ave., Suite 810, Chicago, IL 60601.

**Membership Interests:** As of the Lease Commencement Date, the membership interests in Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC are held as follows:

> • **Dara Okonkwo** --- 60% ownership interest; Managing Member
>
> • **Felix Ramachandran** --- 40% ownership interest; Silent Member (non-managing)

**Authorized Representative:** Dara Okonkwo, in her capacity as Managing Member, is the sole authorized representative of Tenant for all matters arising under or related to the Lease, including the execution of amendments, delivery and receipt of notices, and communications with Landlord.

**Personal Guaranty:** In connection with the execution of the Lease, Dara Okonkwo executed a limited personal guaranty of Tenant\'s obligations under the Lease for the first twenty-four (24) months of the Lease Term. The guaranty is capped at an amount equal to six (6) months of Base Rent (i.e., \$48,000 based on the Year 1--3 Base Rent rate). The terms of the personal guaranty are set forth in Exhibit E to the Lease Agreement.

**[Section 2: Premises Description and Square Footage]{.underline}**

**Premises Address:** 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, Evanston, IL 60201

**Zoning District:** The Premises is located within the C1a Commercial Mixed-Use zoning district as designated by the City of Evanston Zoning Map. The C1a district permits cannabis dispensary operations subject to the issuance of a special use permit or dispensary-specific permit under Chapter 9, Article 5 of the Evanston Municipal Code.

**Total Rentable Square Footage:** 3,200 square feet

**Square Footage Breakdown:** As depicted in Exhibit A to the Lease (Floor Plan and Square Footage Diagram), the Premises is divided into the following functional areas:

  Vestibule / Waiting Area            200 sq. ft.

  **Total**                           **3,200 sq. ft.**
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

**Building Description:** Unit B is located on the ground floor of a two-story mixed-use commercial building. Unit A, the adjacent ground-floor commercial unit, is currently occupied by a retail clothing boutique. The second floor of the building contains residential apartment units. The building is of masonry construction, originally constructed in approximately 1948, with subsequent renovations completed in 2011.

**Rear Access:** The Premises includes a dedicated rear entrance for deliveries and secure product intake, accessible from the building\'s service alley located along the northern boundary of the property. This rear entrance is separate from the primary customer entrance on Sherman Avenue and is designed to facilitate secure cannabis product deliveries in compliance with state and local delivery intake requirements.

**Parking:** Pursuant to Exhibit B to the Lease (Parking Allocation Map), Tenant shall have access to eight (8) designated parking spaces in the rear parking lot, which is shared with other building tenants. The parking allocation satisfies the City of Evanston\'s minimum off-street parking requirements applicable to the Premises based on its square footage and intended commercial use.

**[Section 3: Lease Term and Renewal Options]{.underline}**

**Lease Commencement Date:** January 8, 2024.

**Initial Lease Term:** Seven (7) years, commencing on January 8, 2024, and expiring on January 7, 2031, unless sooner terminated or extended in accordance with the terms of the Lease.

> *First Renewal Term:* January 8, 2031 through January 7, 2034
>
> *Second Renewal Term:* January 8, 2034 through January 7, 2037

**MEMORANDUM**

**TO:** Nadia Petrova, Partner, and Jordan Mackey, Associate Hargrove & Sinclair LLP 77 W. Wacker Drive, Suite 3200 Chicago, IL 60601

**FROM:** Dara Okonkwo, Managing Member, Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC

**DATE:** April 22, 2024

**RE:** Insurance and Bonding Status Summary --- Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application Proposed Dispensary Location: 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, Evanston, IL 60201 State Conditional License No. ADC-285-0147 Target Application Submission Date: May 15, 2024

**CC:** Felix Ramachandran, Silent Member, Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC Marcus Wren, Principal, Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC

**[1. Purpose and Background]{.underline}**

This memorandum summarizes the current status of all insurance policies and bonding or surety arrangements maintained or being pursued by Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC (EIN: 88-3921047), an Illinois limited liability company formed on March 14, 2023, in connection with its application for a City of Evanston cannabis dispensary permit. The City of Evanston application requires evidence of insurance meeting specified minimum coverage thresholds as a condition of permit issuance. In addition, Section 8 of the application form contains a scoring rubric that awards Financial Viability points based, in part, on evidence of secured bonding capacity.

Greenleaf\'s total capitalization stands at \$1,850,000, consisting of \$1,110,000 contributed by Dara Okonkwo and \$740,000 contributed by Felix Ramachandran. The estimated build-out cost for the dispensary premises at 1420 Sherman Avenue, Unit B, Evanston, Illinois 60201 is approximately \$425,000. Projected Year 1 gross revenue is \$3,200,000. Greenleaf\'s banking partner is Ridgeline Community Bank, located at 820 Davis Street, Evanston, IL 60201, which maintains a cannabis banking program requiring quarterly compliance attestations from participating clients.

This memorandum is being provided to outside counsel at Hargrove & Sinclair LLP and to Pinnacle Compliance Advisors LLC for the purpose of identifying any gaps, deficiencies, or action items in Greenleaf\'s insurance and bonding posture prior to the May 15, 2024 application submission deadline and in advance of anticipated permit issuance. We respectfully request counsel\'s guidance on the open items described herein so that Greenleaf can take timely corrective action.

**[2. City of Evanston Insurance Requirements Summary]{.underline}**

Based on our review of the City of Evanston cannabis dispensary permit application materials, the following minimum insurance coverages are required of all applicants:

> • **General Liability:** \$2,000,000 per occurrence / \$4,000,000 aggregate
>
> • **Product Liability:** \$2,000,000 per occurrence / \$4,000,000 aggregate
>
> • **Workers\' Compensation:** Statutory limits as required under the Illinois Workers\' Compensation Act (820 ILCS 305)
>
> • **Cannabis-Specific Crime/Theft Coverage:** \$500,000 minimum

The application materials further require that certificates of insurance naming the City of Evanston as an additional insured be submitted prior to permit issuance. All policies must remain in continuous force throughout the permit period. Any lapse of required coverage constitutes grounds for immediate permit suspension or revocation by the City. Greenleaf understands these requirements and has structured its insurance procurement efforts accordingly, as detailed in the sections that follow.

**[3. Current Insurance Coverage --- Policies Obtained]{.underline}**

The following insurance policies have been obtained and are currently in force or in a compliant posture for purposes of the City of Evanston application.

**3.1 General Liability**

Greenleaf has obtained a commercial general liability policy placed through our commercial insurance broker. The policy provides the following coverage:

> **Limits:** \$2,000,000 per occurrence / \$4,000,000 aggregate
>
> **Effective Date:** January 15, 2024
>
> **Named Insured:** Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC
>
> **Covered Location:** 1420 Sherman Ave., Unit B, Evanston, IL 60201
>
> **Status:** **Compliant** --- meets or exceeds City of Evanston minimums

The general liability policy includes a cannabis dispensary operations endorsement, which expressly extends coverage to the retail sale and handling of cannabis products at the insured location. A certificate of insurance is available for submission and can be endorsed to name the City of Evanston as an additional insured upon request.

**3.2 Product Liability**

Greenleaf\'s product liability coverage is bundled with the general liability policy described in Section 3.1 above.

> **Limits:** \$2,000,000 per occurrence / \$4,000,000 aggregate
>
> **Effective Date:** January 15, 2024
>
> **Status:** **Compliant** --- meets or exceeds City of Evanston minimums

The product liability coverage extends to claims arising from the sale or distribution of cannabis products at the insured dispensary location. A certificate of insurance reflecting this coverage is available for submission with the application.

**3.3 Workers\' Compensation**

> **Coverage:** Statutory limits under the Illinois Workers\' Compensation Act (820 ILCS 305)
>
> **Effective Date:** Policy to be bound upon first employee hire
>
> **Status:** **Compliant in posture** --- Workers\' compensation policy has been quoted and will be bound prior to any employee onboarding; a certificate will be available for submission prior to permit issuance

Greenleaf does not currently have any employees on payroll. The company projects a workforce of approximately twelve (12) full-time employees once the dispensary becomes operational. The projected twelve-employee workforce has been underwritten by our commercial broker, and final premium figures have been quoted based on applicable classification codes for cannabis retail operations in Illinois. The policy will be bound immediately upon the first emplo

turns.name: read
turns.result:
"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

tool_calls.name: bash
tool_calls.cmd: which pandoc && pandoc --version | head -1; echo "---templates---"; ls $WORKSPACE_DIR/skills/docx/ 2>/dev/null; find $WORKSPACE_DIR/skills -name "*.docx" 2>/dev/null | head

Compliance Requirements Matrix

City of Evanston Cannabis Dispensary Permit Application — Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC

Prepared by: Outside Cannabis Licensing Counsel Re: Review of the
Greenleaf Therapeutics LLC dispensary permit application package against
the Evanston Municipal Code, Title 9, Article 5, and the City’s
Application Checklist and Submission Guide Proposed premises: 1420
Sherman Ave., Unit B, Evanston, IL 60201 Applicant entity: Greenleaf
Therapeutics LLC (Illinois LLC; EIN 88-3921047) Target submission date:
May 15, 2024 (within the February 1 – May 31, 2024 application window)

1. Executive Summary

Counsel reviewed the eight documents that comprise the Greenleaf
Therapeutics LLC (“Greenleaf”) application package and measured each
against the controlling authority: Evanston Municipal Code Title 9,
Article 5 (“Article 5” or the “Ordinance”) and the City’s Cannabis
Dispensary Permit Application Checklist and Submission Guide (the
“Guide”). The review produced a requirements matrix of 51 distinct
requirement lines, organized into eleven thematic groups, together with
a catalog of cross-document discrepancies between the Ordinance, the
Guide, and the official Application Form, and a critical timeline
analysis.

Overall posture. The application is substantively strong in the areas
the applicant has prioritized — business plan, social equity narrative,
community engagement outreach, and lease/site control. However, the
package is not yet submission-ready. Several items that the Ordinance
and Guide require to be submitted at the time of application are missing
or incomplete, and at least three of those gaps are of a kind that the
Cannabis Licensing Coordinator is directed to treat as rendering the
application incomplete.

Critical findings (must be resolved before submission):

1.  Odor Mitigation Plan lacks the required Professional Engineer
    certification. The Ordinance (§9-5-5(B)(10)), the Guide (Item 10),
    and the Application Form (Q16) all require a signed/sealed
    certification by an Illinois-licensed P.E. at the time of
    submission. The business plan expressly states that HVAC and
    filtration specifications “will be finalized during the build-out
    engineering phase.” The Form warns that an application submitted
    without the P.E. certification “will be deemed incomplete and will
    not proceed to scoring.”
2.  No evidence of bonding capacity / commitment letter. Only a
    “preliminary inquiry” and a “verbal” indication from Thornfield
    Surety Group exist. The Guide (Item 17) and Form (Q26) require a
    commitment letter at submission; absent it, the applicant scores
    zero of ten in the bonding-capacity sub-category of Financial
    Viability.
3.  State conditional license expiration timeline. Conditional License
    ADC-285-0147 expires September 21, 2024. On the City’s own 120–150
    day processing estimate, a May 15, 2024 submission yields permit
    issuance in October–November 2024 — after the State license has
    lapsed. No petition for the State’s permitted six-month extension
    has been documented. This jeopardizes both the local permit
    (§9-5-11(I)) and the State conversion.
4.  Community Engagement Session 2 notice was only 12 days. Notice for
    the April 9, 2024 session was published March 28, 2024 — 12 calendar
    days, short of the 14-day minimum in §9-5-8(B). The applicant’s own
    report records “12 days” and omits the compliance attestation it
    supplied for Session 1. Under §9-5-8(F) this renders the application
    incomplete and requires an additional, fully-noticed session; the
    two-session minimum is non-waivable.

Major findings (curable, but must be cured within the deficiency
period): missing principal-officer disclosure forms, background-check
authorizations, and fingerprint receipts; a stale (December 15, 2023)
buffer survey that also uses straight-line rather than the
pedestrian-route methodology required by the Form; no evidence that the
special-use application has been filed; no standalone Environmental
Sustainability, Waste Disposal, or Diversity & Inclusion plans in the
package; no documentation of the 24-month R3 residency duration or of
the expungement order; cannabis crime/theft insurance not bound and no
commitment letter; the official Application Form is supplied as a blank
template and is not completed.

Cross-document discrepancies. The Ordinance, the Guide, and the
Application Form disagree on a number of points that directly affect the

turns.text:
The deliverable is complete. Here is a summary of what I produced.

## Deliverable: `output/requirements-matrix.docx`

I reviewed all eight documents in the package — the **Evanston Municipal Code, Title 9, Article 5** (controlling law), the **Application Checklist and Submission Guide** (submission standard), the **official Application Form** (supplied blank), and the five applicant submissions (business plan, lease summary, insurance/bonding memo, State conditional license, and community-engagement report) — and produced a comprehensive compliance requirements matrix.

### Structure of the matrix
- **Executive summary** with the four critical findings
- **Documents reviewed** and **legend** (status + severity definitions)
- **Compliance Requirements Matrix** — 51 requirement lines across 11 thematic groups (A–K), each with authority citation, submission status, assessment, severity, and concrete remediation
- **Part B — Cross-document discrepancies** between the Ordinance, Guide, and Form (12 conflicts, e.g., 30-day vs. 14-day cure period; retail-floor vs. total-sq-ft occupancy formula; property-line vs. pedestrian-route buffer measurement)
- **Part C — Critical timeline analysis** of the State conditional license expiration
- **Part D — Scoring impact** by rubric category
- **Part E — Prioritized remediation action plan** (Tiers 1–4)

### Key findings surfaced
1. **CRITICAL — Missing P.E. odor certification** (specs "will be finalized during build-out"; Form says application "will not proceed to scoring" without it)
2. **CRITICAL — No bonding commitment letter** (only a verbal indication; costs 10 Financial Viability points)
3. **CRITICAL — State conditional license expires Sept. 21, 2024**, before the local permit can realistically issue; no extension petition documented
4. **CRITICAL — Session 2 notice was only 12 days** (short of the 14-day minimum; §9-5-8(F) requires an additional fully-noticed session)
5. Plus major gaps: blank official Form, missing principal-officer/background-check disclosures, stale straight-line survey, no filed special-use application, no standalone environmental/waste/diversity plans, unconfirmed 24-month R3 residency and expungement docs, unbound crime/theft insurance, and several internal inconsistencies (registered-agent name, phone/email, parking count).

The .docx was generated via Pandoc and passed the mandatory `validate.py` schema/integrity check.
